L1.area: API service authentication and authorization layer

L2.description: An improper authorization vulnerability exists where, in multi-tenant deployments, the API service does not verify that the issuer claim in a bearer token belongs to an allowed identity provider tenant. This allows an authenticated user from any tenant to make authorized API calls, resulting in unauthorized read/write access to private data such as vulnerability information, security testing tools, and proprietary code, as well as enabling tampering with existing data and unauthorized code execution on cloud compute resources.

L3.area: API service authentication and authorization layer
L3.description: An improper authorization vulnerability exists where, in multi-tenant deployments, the API service does not verify that the issuer claim in a bearer token belongs to an allowed identity provider tenant. This allows an authenticated user from any tenant to make authorized API calls, resulting in unauthorized read/write access to private data such as vulnerability information, security testing tools, and proprietary code, as well as enabling tampering with existing data and unauthorized code execution on cloud compute resources.

ground_truth.reason: An incomplete authorization check in multi-tenant deployments fails to verify the bearer token's issuer against an allowlist of permitted AAD tenants, allowing any authenticated Azure AD user to make authorized API calls.
